mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of rate increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer income, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based domestic demand growth, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative mean forward return over 60 days, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.02 compared to single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply chain efficiency, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of recovery period observations, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 10 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL crosses above 2.5 percent annualized threshold
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of high-inflation trigger events, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher nonfarm payroll employment levels reflect robust economic activity and sustained wage growth, which directly supports corporate revenue projections and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The series exhibits a negative rank correlation with 20-day forward returns over any 24-month rolling window, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate synchronized strength in durable goods demand and consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal generates negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising consumer prices signals a supply-side shock that erodes corporate margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during stagflationary periods.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns, or equity markets remain flat or positive in more than 70% of stagflationary trigger instances.
